qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection via unsanitized $_GET['righe'] in confronta_righe.php modal allows authenticated data exfiltration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability: the advisory describes SQL injection in ajax_select.php via the componenti operation and options[matricola] parameter, while this finding identifies SQL injection in confronta_righe.php modal files via the righe parameter. Different endpoints, parameters, and code locations.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OS command injection via unsanitized filename in XML::decodeP7M() exec() call during electronic invoice import
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class: the advisory describes SQL injection, while this finding identifies OS command injection. Completely different vulnerability type, root cause, and attack vector.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes SQL injection in ajax_select.php via the componenti operation and options[matricola] parameter. The two qualifying findings are: (1) SQL injection in confronta_righe.php via the righe parameter - different endpoint and parameter, (2) Command injection in XML::decodeP7M() - completely different vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable parameter: options[matricola] in ajax_select.php endpoint with componenti operation
sql_injection_righe_parameter vulnerable location: modules/*/modals/confronta_righe.php via righe parameter - different code path
command_injection_p7m_decode: Command injection vulnerability - different vulnerability class (CWE-78 vs CWE-89)
